title: Weights & Biases Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of experiment tracking, hyperparameter sweeps, and artifact management with W&B.

questions.question: What does `wandb.init(config={...})` primarily accomplish when called at the start of a training run?

options.text: It trains the model using the hyperparameters in the config dict
It creates a new W&B run, stores all hyperparameters, and begins streaming metrics to the cloud dashboard
It downloads the latest dataset artifact from W&B storage
It sets up a distributed training job across multiple GPUs

questions.question: What is the main advantage of W&B Sweeps over manual hyperparameter search?

options.text: Sweeps train models faster by using multiple GPUs
Sweeps automatically manage the search over your hyperparameter space using Bayesian optimization, grid search, or random search
Sweeps automatically deploy the best model to production
Sweeps only work with scikit-learn models

questions.question: In a W&B Sweep with `method: 'bayes'`, how does it decide which hyperparameters to try next?

options.text: It tries all combinations exhaustively from the search space
It picks random values from the specified distributions on each run
It uses the results of previous runs to build a probabilistic model that predicts which hyperparameters are most likely to improve the target metric
It clones the hyperparameters from the best existing run and adds random noise

questions.question: What is a W&B Artifact, and why is it important for reproducibility?

options.text: A chart widget embedded in a W&B Report
A versioned package of files (models, datasets) with tracked lineage — showing which dataset version trained which model version
The set of hyperparameters used in a specific run
An alert notification sent when a metric exceeds a threshold

questions.question: Where should you store your W&B API key in a production/CI environment?

options.text: Hardcode it in the training script for convenience
In a public GitHub repository so all team members can access it
As the WANDB_API_KEY environment variable, injected via CI secrets or a secrets manager
In the model's config.yaml file that is committed to git
